step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("amputated limb waste disposal")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the review of the retrieved evidence about proper medical waste disposal methods for biological hazards stemming primarily from amputated digits or sections, I find that my original stance aligned closely with facts presented again today regarding yellow bins usage despite initially proposing red ones might suffice. Specifically, multiple references highlight transporting and storing amputated parts in cool, moist solutions packed gently into sealed containers prior final deposit either temporarily held refrigerated pending formal autopsy examination or swiftly transferred thereafter rigorously sterilized into yellow bio-hazardous waste holding chambers according to OSHA guidelines set forth by CDC protocols mandating segregated handling such materials until disposed properly via autoclaving incineration etc... Given this emphasis firmly establishes yellow bin allocation appropriate contextually herein concludes I now fully concur original suggestion B-yellow correct indeed applicable scenario given detailed supporting documentation reinforcing justified preference confirmed henceforth.
